name: Amadòrio reservoir and views of Puig Campana: loop from Benidorm
name_original: Embalse de Amadòrio y vistas al Puig Campana — Circular desde Benidorm
description:
From the vibrant city of Benidorm and its seafront, through the wild landscape of the Amadòrio River and the spectacular views of Puig Campana, this route has a bit of everything. You leave Benidorm train station and go down to the coast, ride along the beach and start the climb towards the mountains.

The route gradually leaves the intense urban area and climbs along the Amadòrio River while enjoying the views of its ravine. You cross the reservoir dam and, as you skirt the water, you reach a viewing point from which you can see the mountains and the Mediterranean forest. A little further on you find Bella Orcheta where you can refill water and stock up on supplies.

As you continue up the river ravine, the landscape becomes increasingly wild and the reddish and ochre tones of the land become vibrant. There are even some easy stretches of trail waiting for you to savour nature even more. Almost in the second third of the route, you pass through Finestrat, a steep town where you can take a break and recharge your batteries in any of its bars. From here, the presence of the majestic Puig Campana accompanies you until you return to Benidorm.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Benidorm offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region is characterized by coastal mountain ranges such as Sierra Helada, iconic peaks like Puig Campana, and inland valleys featuring reservoirs such as Amadòrio and Guadalest. This varied terrain includes a mix of unpaved gravel lanes, dedicated bike paths, and quiet roads, providing options for different riding preferences. The area's geographical position between mountains and the Mediterranean Sea creates a range of elevation changes and scenic viewpoints. Best gravel bike trails around Benidorm The most popular gravel bike trail is Amadòrio reservoir and views of Puig Campana: loop from Benidorm, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of Puig Campana and follows the Amadòrio Reservoir. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Blick on Guadalest – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benidorm Intermodal, a difficult 38.0 miles (61.2 km) path. This route features the scenic Guadalest Valley and its reservoir, with significant elevation gain. Local gravel bikers also love the Comptador Pass – Font del Molí loop from Benidorm, a 34.7 miles (55.8 km) trail leading through challenging mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Gravel biking around Benidorm is defined by coastal mountain ranges, iconic peaks, and scenic reservoirs, offering a mix of challenging climbs and varied terrain. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 300 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Benidorm?
A: There are over 20 gravel bike trails around Benidorm, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 300 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm's gravel trails feature a diverse mix of terrain, from quiet paved roads and dedicated bike paths to unpaved gravel lanes, some with loose stones. You'll encounter challenging climbs through mountainous areas like Sierra Helada and Puig Campana, as well as scenic paths around reservoirs such as Amadòrio and Guadalest.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in Benidorm?
A: While many routes in Benidorm are rated as difficult, the region does offer options that combine smoother sections with light gravel. For a less strenuous experience, consider routes that focus on the flatter sections around the reservoirs, though these may still include some elevation changes. Currently, there are no routes explicitly rated as 'easy' in the guide, but some moderate sections can be found.

Q: What are the most scenic gravel bike routes in Benidorm?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes that loop around the region's natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922603334/amadorio-reservoir-and-views-of-puig-campana-loop-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadòrio reservoir and views of Puig Campana: loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas of Puig Campana and the reservoir. Another highly scenic option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blick on Guadalest – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the picturesque Guadalest Valley.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes around Benidorm?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike trails in Benidorm are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922603334/amadorio-reservoir-and-views-of-puig-campana-loop-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadòrio reservoir and views of Puig Campana: loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comptador Pass – Font del Molí loop from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: Benidorm's gravel trails often pass by significant natural features. You can ride alongside the clear blue waters of the Amadòrio and Guadalest Reservoirs, and enjoy views of iconic peaks like Puig Campana. The region also features the dramatic Sierra Helada Natural Park. For specific points of interest, consider exploring highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu Footbridge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about cycling in Benidorm?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 15 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, which offers a mix of challenging climbs and varied surfaces, as well as the stunning coastal and mountain views. The accessibility to natural parks like Sierra Helada from the city center is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any long-distance gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Benidorm offers several challenging long-distance routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blick on Guadalest – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt; covers over 38 miles (61 km) with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31021080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí – View of the Mountain Peak loop from Terra Mítica&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 34 miles (55 km) long and features substantial climbing.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm's mild climate makes it an attractive destination for cycling year-round. However, the cooler months outside of peak summer, such as spring and autumn, generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides and challenging climbs, avoiding the intense heat of July and August.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Benidorm?
A: While many of the listed routes are rated as difficult, families might find shorter, less technical sections around the Amadòrio Reservoir suitable for a more relaxed ride. These areas often feature gravel paths along the water with gentler gradients. Always check the route details for elevation and surface type to ensure it matches your family's skill level.

Q: Where can I find parking near the gravel bike trailheads in Benidorm?
A: Many routes start from Benidorm itself or nearby areas. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blick on Guadalest – Guadalest Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Dam – Amadorio Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;, starting points like Benidorm Intermodal or areas near Terra Mítica often have parking facilities available, though it's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability beforehand.
tips: Beautifully landscaped promenade along the Poniente Beach of Benidorm

Very nice view of the reservoir and the Puig Campana in the background. The reservoir near Orxeta (Embalse de Amadorio) is an ideal destination for cyclists who want to explore the area. The landscape around the reservoir offers varied routes with breathtaking views and a peaceful atmosphere

The Puente del Río Amadorio is a historic bridge built for transport and as a connection between the towns. A complete circuit around the reservoir is worthwhile. The bridge spans the Amadorio River, and on the other side, you drive over the dam. A very scenic route.
This is a narrow stretch of trail that is not difficult at all and is very pleasant. The terrain is very good and can also be done on a gravel bike.

The landscape around Puig Campana is a real highlight and a worthwhile destination for a tour! You will inevitably also drive through Finestrat, a very beautiful mountain village worth seeing! Puig Campana is a 1408 m high mountain in the province of Alicante. It belongs to the Cordillera Penibética mountain range.

waypoints.name: View of the Embalse de Amadorio
